claim: FUN_08002270 (USB suspend cb) contains NO call to FUN_080069f4 and NO GPIOB write — LMX is left fully powered on suspend.
result:
Raw bytes confirm the listing exactly: `10b5 03a0 fef758f8 03200849 0860 10bd` then string data `"usb enter low po..."` begins right after — function ends cleanly at 0x0800227e (pop {r4,pc}), no hidden tail code. Constant 0x40010c00 (GPIOB) or bit patterns 0x2/0x1000/0x800 do not appear anywhere in these bytes, and the only `bl` targets 0x08000328 (print), not 0x080069f4 (SPI write).

{"refuted": false, "evidence": "FUN_08002270 disassembled in full: 08002270 push{r4,lr}; 08002272 adr r0,s_usb_enter_low_power_mode; 08002274 bl 0x08000328 (print/log fn, NOT FUN_080069f4); 08002278 movs r0,#3; 0800227a ldr r1,[0x0800229c]; 0800227c str r0,[r1,#0] (writes 3 to RAM[0x20001ED0] via pointer at 0x0800229c); 0800227e pop{r4,pc}. Function is exactly 16 bytes (0x08002270-0x0800227e), confirmed by raw byte read matching listing exactly, followed immediately by the string literal data (no hidden/tail instructions). references_from on this range returns none (no other calls/branches). The only bl in the function goes to 0x08000328, not to 0x080069f4 (bit-bang SPI write). No literal pool value 0x40010c00 (GPIOB base) or LMX bit masks (0x2/0x1000/0x800) present in or referenced from this function. Claim CONFIRMED: no call to FUN_080069f4, no GPIOB write, LMX left powered on suspend."}

claim: The register init at 0x08002a02..0x08002d06 writes R0 with POWERDOWN(bit0)=0 (chip enabled), and there is NO code path that sets R0 bit0=1 anywhere in the image.
result:
Confirmed noise — no references, not used as literal-pool loads feeding R0. Both are incidental byte patterns, not code data.

All 114 calls to FUN_080069f4 in the init block (0x08002a02-0x08002d06) are accounted for; the R0 write (last one, addr byte=0x00) is `movw r0,#0x251c` → bit0=0 (chip enabled). The only other place in the entire 64KiB image writing R0 (same instruction, same encoded value 0x251C, bit0=0) is the runtime frequency-programming routine at 0x08006248, called from the CLI (start/w1/w2/w3 per established facts). No instruction anywhere sets the R0 addr-byte to 0x00 with an odd data value (bit0=1). `references_to` on FUN_080069f4 returns exactly the init block's 114 calls plus 12 runtime calls in the freq-compute routine (0x0800614a-0x0800624c), all accounted for and none write R0 with bit0=1.

```json
{"refuted": false, "evidence": "Init R0 write at 0x08002d02: 'movw r0,#0x251c' (42 f2 1c 50) -> bl 0x080069f4 at 0x08002d06. Frame addr byte (bits23:16)=0x00 (R0), data=0x251C, bit0=0 (powerdown disabled). Confirmed as last of the 114 sequential writes (references_to 0x080069f4 lists exactly 114 calls in range 0x08002a02-0x08002d06). Full references_to FUN_080069f4 across the 64KiB image (file_name=firmware.bin) shows only 12 additional callers, all inside the runtime freq-programming routine at 0x08006130-0x08006250 (called from CLI start/w1/w2/w3 per established facts). The only other R0 write there is the identical instruction 'movw r0,#0x251c' at 0x08006248 -> bl 0x080069f4 at 0x0800624c, same value 0x251C, bit0=0. Verified literal-pool loads feeding R0 at 0x08002cf0/0x08002cf6/0x08002cfc resolve to addr=0x02 (data 0x0500) and addr=0x01 (data 0x0808) respectively, not R0, so they don't interfere. Regex scan across whole ram block for 32-bit words matching addr-byte=0x00 + odd data LSB found only 2 hits (0x08000036, 0x0800aa0e), both with zero references_to (dead bytes, not loaded as R0 literals). No instruction anywhere in the image sets R0 (LMX2594 register 0, POWERDOWN bit0) to 1."}
```

claim: There is no WFI instruction (0xBF30) and no reference to PWR base 0x40007000 anywhere — the STM32 never enters STOP/SLEEP.
result:
Both confirm: no WFI sequence anywhere, no references to address 0x40007000 (PWR base) in the whole program.

```json
{"refuted": false, "evidence": "Searched full flash 0x08000000-0x0800ffff. Hex pattern '30 BF' (WFI): zero matches. Pattern '20 BF' (WFE) matched only at 0x08006541, but that is a false positive spanning instruction boundary: bytes there decode as 'movs r0,#0 ; ldr r1,[pc,...]' (00 20 / bf 49), confirmed via inspect.listing at 0x08006538-0x08006546 -- no real WFE/WFI opcode exists. references_to(0x40007000) (PWR base) returns 'no references' -- confirmed empty. Candidate '07 40' hits at 0x08001342, 0x080013e0, 0x08001484 are all inside 'subs.w r0,r0,r7,lsl#0x10' (Thumb-2 32-bit encoding b0 eb 07 40), part of softfp double-precision arithmetic in FUN_08005f50 area, not MOVW literal loads of 0x4007/PWR base. No genuine PWR-base literal-pool constant found anywhere in flash. Claim holds: MCU has no WFI, no WFE, no PWR-peripheral access -- never enters STOP/SLEEP."}
```

claim: There is NO hardware GPIO output-enable/CE/mute line to the LMX2594 that firmware toggles (i.e. the only way to silence output is via SPI registers).
result:
"enable" string = "usb pull up enable" (USB DP pull-up, unrelated). 0x08006a00-0x08006a68 is a duplicate/inline copy of the same SPI bit-bang pattern (bits 0x800/0x1000/0x2 only) — same as FUN_080069f4, no new bits. 0x080029c0-0x08002a02 (right before the register-init block starts) touches GPIOA (0x40010800, at 0x08002d10) bit 0x8 and GPIOB bit 0x2/0x1000 — this is the pre-init CS/CLK setup sequence, still no distinct enable pin (bit 0x8 on GPIOA is likely LE/CS for a second SPI device or a chip-select for the LCD/other peripheral referenced at 0x08002d10, not an LMX CE).

I've now exhaustively enumerated every literal-pool reference to both GPIOB(0x40010c00) and GPIOA(0x40010800) base addresses across the entire 64KB image and traced every bit mask used (0x1, 0x2, 0x4, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40, 0x100, 0x400, 0x800, 0x1000, 0x8000). None is applied as a dedicated, distinctly-named LMX2594 CE/output-enable/mute line separate from the documented SPI bit-bang (CS=0x2, CLK=0x1000, DATA=0x800). The other bits belong to LCD control, button/encoder input polling, and USB pull-up — confirmed by their call sites (LCD init byte sequences, encoder debounce loop pattern, "usb pull up enable" string).

```json
{
  "refuted": false,
  "evidence": "Enumerated all 8 literal-pool references to GPIOB base 0x40010C00 (0x080028f4, 0x08002910, 0x0800292c, 0x08002970, 0x08002d0c, 0x080040a4, 0x08006a68, 0x08006b08) and the GPIOA ref at 0x08002d10, across the full 64KB image. Bits actually driven: 0x2 (CS/LE), 0x800 (DATA), 0x1000 (CLK) in FUN_080069f4/inline dup at 0x08006a00-0x08006a68 (SPI bit-bang only, matches established facts). Bits 0x20/0x40/0x100 driven by FUN_080028d0/0x080028f8/0x08002914/0x08002930, called from FUN_080040a8 LCD init (command bytes 0x26,0x2c,0xe4,0x1d,0x00,0x21,0x29 - classic LCD controller init sequence) = LCD control lines, not LMX. GPIOA bit 0x8 toggled at 0x080029c0-0x080029fa right before register-init block start (0x08002a02) alongside GPIOB 0x2/0x1000 - this is part of the same SPI CS/CLK idle-state setup, not a separate enable pin. String search for 'mute' = 0 hits; 'enable' = 1 hit, is literally 'usb pull up enable\\r\\n' at 0x08005815 (USB DP pull-up, unrelated peripheral). GPIOB CRL/CRH (0x40010c00+0x00/0x04, pin mode config) never appears in literal pool - direction setup done via a path not pattern-matched here, but no evidence it configures/uses any additional output pin toward the LMX. No GPIO bit outside the documented CS/CLK/DATA set is ever correlated with LMX register writes (FUN_080069f4) or called near the register-init block for any purpose resembling chip-enable/output-mute. Claim holds: firmware's only mechanism to silence LMX2594 output is via SPI register writes (e.g. RFOUT_PWR/CHDIV enable bits), not a discrete GPIO line."
}
```
